question: Tell me about a time you disagreed with a teammate.
transcript: I disagreed with my teammate about the architecture. We sat down, mapped out both approaches on a whiteboard, and realized a hybrid approach was best. It shipped on time and worked great.

question: Tell me about a time you disagreed with a teammate.
transcript: Well, my teammate was just completely wrong and wouldn't listen to me. So I went to the manager and told him the guy was an idiot. We ended up doing it my way.

question: Tell me about a time you disagreed with a teammate.
transcript: I don't really disagree with people often. I usually just do whatever the team wants to keep the peace. Conflict is bad.

question: Tell me about a time you disagreed with a teammate.
transcript: One time we had a disagreement over which database to use. I wanted Postgres, they wanted MongoDB. I created a small benchmark script to test our specific payload. The data showed Postgres was 3x faster, so we went with that.

question: Tell me about a time you disagreed with a teammate.
transcript: Um, yeah. So, one time, we, uh, couldn't decide on the color of the button. So we just made it blue.

question: Tell me about a time you disagreed with a teammate.
transcript: During my internship, a senior dev wanted to hardcode values to save time. I disagreed because it would cause tech debt. I proposed a config file approach, explained the long-term benefit, and he agreed.
